records.ety: [OE.comin, AS. cymen, fr. L. cuminum, Gr. <?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/<?/; of Semitic origin, cf. Ar. kamm<?/n, Heb. kamm<?/n; cf. OF. comin, F. cumin. Cf. Kummel.]

defs: A dwarf umbelliferous plant, somewhat resembling fennel (Cuminum Cyminum), cultivated for its seeds, which have a bitterish, warm taste, with an aromatic flavor, and are used like those of anise and caraway.
quotes: Rank-smelling rue, and cumin good for eyes.
